topic: A brief history of money — before coins and cash

beats.narration: For most of history, there was no money — only what you could trade by hand.

shots.scene: two traders exchanging a goat cutout and grain sacks, clay tokens, shells, kraft tag BEFORE MONEY
shots.element_motion: goat bobs, tokens scatter, tape flutters

shots.scene: close-up of hands exchanging shells and clay tokens on torn paper map scrap

beats.narration: Barter only works when both sides want exactly what the other has — a fragile coincidence.

shots.scene: split collage: farmer with wheat, smith with tools, red X stickers on mismatched wants
shots.element_motion: red X stickers stamp down, arrows bounce between sides

beats.narration: Shells, salt, and metal pieces became shared standards — value you could carry.

shots.scene: cowrie shells, salt blocks, copper rings as labeled paper cutouts on map board
shots.element_motion: shells rotate, salt blocks stack, ring spins

beats.narration: Stamped coins locked weight and trust into a single object — money as we know it began.

shots.scene: ancient stamped coin hero cutout, wax seal, minting hammer, banner FIRST COINS
shots.element_motion: seal stamps down, coin settles with paper jitter

beats.narration: From clay tokens to cards and code — money keeps changing shape. The need to exchange never did.

shots.scene: timeline strip: clay token → coin → paper bill → card → phone screen, all paper cutouts
shots.element_motion: timeline pieces snap in left to right
